content.text:
Good news: that statistic is basically a myth. You're not doomed to die 9 years early because you write with your left hand. Let me explain what's actually going on.

## Where the "9 years" claim came from

There was a study back in 1991 (by Halpern and Coren) that looked at death records in California. They asked the families of dead people whether their relative was left- or right-handed, then compared the average age of death. The lefties came out younger, and the number "9 years" got stuck in people's heads forever.

## Why the study was flawed

Here's the simple version of what went wrong:

**The big mistake was about *old* people, not dead ones.**

A long time ago, being left-handed was treated as a bad thing. Teachers, parents, and even nuns would force left-handed kids to switch to using their right hand. Some people were even punished for it.

So if you look at someone who was 80 or 90 years old in 1991, many of them were *born* left-handed but were forced to act right-handed their whole lives. When researchers asked their family, "Was Grandpa left-handed?" the family said "No, he was right-handed!"—because that's all they ever saw.

This means:
- **Older generations had very few "official" lefties** (they were forced to switch).
- **Younger generations had lots of lefties** (society stopped forcing the switch).

So when you average it out, lefties *look* like they die younger—but really it's just that most of the visible lefties were young to begin with. It's a measurement trick, not a health truth.

## The simple analogy

Imagine you visit a school and notice that all the kids with brand-new sneakers are short, and all the kids with old worn-out sneakers are tall. You might wrongly conclude "new sneakers make you short!"

But the real reason is that the school just started selling new sneakers this year, so only the younger (shorter) kids have them. The sneakers have nothing to do with height. Same idea with lefties and lifespan.

## The bottom line

Modern, better-designed studies have found **no meaningful difference in lifespan** between left- and right-handed people. So relax—you're going to be just fine. 🙂
